# kubernetes_grpc_route_v1 (Resource)

GRPCRoute provides a way to route gRPC requests.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `metadata` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Standard grpcroute_v1's metadata. More info: https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--metadata))
- `spec` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Spec defines the desired state of GRPCRoute. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec))

### Optional

- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `status` (List of Object) Status defines the current state of GRPCRoute. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--status))

<a id="nestedblock--metadata"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metadata`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) An unstructured key value map stored with the grpcroute_v1 that may be used to store arbitrary metadata. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `generate_name` (String) Prefix, used by the server, to generate a unique name ONLY IF the `name` field has not been provided. This value will also be combined with a unique suffix. More info: https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#idempotency
- `labels` (Map of String) Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize (scope and select) the grpcroute_v1. May match selectors of replication controllers and services. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `name` (String) Name of the grpcroute_v1, must be unique. Cannot be updated. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names
- `namespace` (String) Namespace defines the space within which name of the grpcroute_v1 must be unique.

Read-Only:

- `generation` (Number) A sequence number representing a specific generation of the desired state.
- `resource_version` (String) An opaque value that represents the internal version of this grpcroute_v1 that can be used by clients to determine when grpcroute_v1 has changed. More info: https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#concurrency-control-and-consistency
- `uid` (String) The unique in time and space value for this grpcroute_v1. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#uids


<a id="nestedblock--spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec`

Optional:

- `hostnames` (List of String) Hostnames defines a set of hostnames that should match against the GRPC Host header.
- `parent_refs` (Block List) ParentRefs identifies an API object (usually a Gateway) that routes should reference to attach to it. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--parent_refs))
- `rules` (Block List) Rules are a list of GRPC matchers, filters and actions. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--rules))
- `use_default_gateways` (String) UseDefaultGateways indicates the default Gateway scope to use for this Route.

<a id="nestedblock--spec--parent_refs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.parent_refs`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name is the name of the referent.

Optional:

- `group` (String) Group is the group of the referent. Defaults to gateway.networking.k8s.io.
- `kind` (String) Kind is the kind of the referent. Defaults to Gateway.
- `namespace` (String) Namespace is the namespace of the referent.
- `port` (Number) Port is the network port this Route targets.
- `section_name` (String) SectionName is the name of a section within the target resource (e.g., a listener name).


<a id="nestedblock--spec--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.rules`

Optional:

- `backend_refs` (Block List) BackendRefs defines the backend(s) where matching requests should be sent. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs))
- `filters` (Block List) Filters define the filters that are applied to requests that match this rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--rules--filters))
- `matches` (Block List) Matches define conditions used for matching the rule against incoming gRPC requests. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--rules--matches))
- `name` (String) Name is the name of the route rule.
- `session_persistence` (Block List, Max: 1) SessionPersistence defines and configures session persistence for the route rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--rules--session_persistence))

<a id="nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.rules.backend_refs`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name is the name of the referent.
- `port` (Number) Port is the port number of the referent.

Optional:

- `filters` (Block List) Filters defined at this level are applied before any rule-level filters. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs--filters))
- `group` (String) Group is the group of the referent.
- `kind` (String) Kind is the kind of the referent.
- `namespace` (String) Namespace is the namespace of the referent.
- `weight` (Number) Weight specifies the proportion of requests.

<a id="nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs--filters"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.rules.backend_refs.filters`

Required:

- `type` (String) Type is the type of filter.

Optional:

- `extension_ref` (Block List, Max: 1) ExtensionRef is a reference to a custom extension filter. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs--filters--extension_ref))
- `request_header_modifier` (Block List, Max: 1) HeaderModifier modifies request or response headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs--filters--request_header_modifier))
- `request_mirror` (Block List, Max: 1) RequestMirror mirrors requests to another backend. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs--filters--request_mirror))
- `response_header_modifier` (Block List, Max: 1) HeaderModifier modifies request or response headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs--filters--response_header_modifier))

<a id="nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs--filters--extension_ref"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.rules.backend_refs.filters.extension_ref`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name is the name of the extension.


<a id="nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs--filters--request_header_modifier"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.rules.backend_refs.filters.request_header_modifier`

Optional:

- `add` (Block List) Add adds headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs--filters--request_header_modifier--add))
- `remove` (List of String) Remove removes headers.
- `set` (Block List) Set overwrites the headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs--filters--request_header_modifier--set))

<a id="nestedblock--spec--rules--backend_refs--filters--request_header_modifier--add"></a>

<a id="nestedblock--spec--rules--matches"></a>

Optional:

<a id="nestedblock--spec--rules--matches--method"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.rules.matches.method`

Optional:

- `method` (String) Method is the gRPC method name.
- `service` (String) Service is the gRPC service name.
- `type` (String) Type specifies how to match against the service and/or method.



<a id="nestedblock--spec--rules--session_persistence"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.rules.session_persistence`

Optional:

- `absolute_timeout` (String) AbsoluteTimeout specifies the maximum duration for the session.
- `idle_timeout` (String) IdleTimeout specifies the duration after which an idle session should be expired.
- `session_name` (String) SessionName is the name of the session.
- `type` (String) Type defines the type of session persistence.
